David’s instruction to Solomon is to keep God’s charge. A dying father’s authority is significant, but nothing compared to that of a living God. God promised David that the Messiah would come from his descendants, and that promise was absolute; but the promise that there would not fail of them a man on the throne of Israel was conditional: if he walks before God in sincerity, with zeal and resolution. To this end, he must take heed to his way.

These dying counsels concerning Joab and Shimei did not come from personal anger, but for the security of Solomon’s throne, because of the murders they had committed, but would readily repeat them to carry any purpose; though long reprieved, they shall be held accountable at last. Time does not wear out the guilt of any sin, particularly of murder. Concerning Shimei, do not hold him guiltless; do not think him a true friend to you or your government, or fit to be trusted; he has no less malice now than he had then. David’s dying sentiments are recorded as delivered under the influence of the Holy Ghost. The Lord revealed to him the offices and the salvation of that glorious personage, the Messiah, whose coming he then foretold, and from whom he derived all his comforts and expectations. That passage gives clear proof that David died under the influence of the Holy Ghost, in the exercise of faith and hope.

Solomon received Bathsheba with all the respect due to a mother; but do not ask for what you ought not to grant. It does not become a good man to prefer a bad request or appear in a bad cause. According to eastern customs, it was plain that Adonijah sought to be king by asking for Abishag as his wife, and Solomon could not be safe while he lived. Ambitious, turbulent spirits commonly prepare death for themselves. Many a head has been lost by reaching for a crown.

Solomon’s words to Abiathar, and his silence, imply that some recent conspiracies had been entered into. Those who show kindness to God's people will have it remembered to their advantage. For this reason Solomon spares Abiathar’s life but dismisses him from his offices. In case of sins for which the blood of beasts would atone, the altar was a refuge, but not in Joab’s case. Solomon looks upward to God as the Author of peace and forward to eternity as its perfection. The Lord of peace himself gives us that peace which is everlasting.

The old malice remains in the unconverted heart, and a watchful eye should be kept on those who, like Shimei, have manifested their enmity but have given no evidence of repentance. No engagements or dangers will restrain worldly men; they go on, even though they forfeit their lives and souls. Let us remember that God will not accommodate his judgment to us. His eye is over us, and let us strive to walk as in his presence. Let our every act, word, and thought be governed by this great truth: the hour is quickly coming when the smallest circumstances of our lives shall be brought to light, and our eternal state be fixed by a righteous and unerring God. Thus Solomon’s throne was established in peace, as a type of the Redeemer's kingdom of peace and righteousness. And it is a comfort, in reference to the enmity of the church’s enemies, that, however much they rage, it is a vain thing they imagine. Christ’s throne is established, and they cannot shake it.
